tex:
Sean is the manager of a museum.
\newline

(a) He buys a Chinese pot costing 1200 yuan.
\newline
The exchange rate is \$1 = 6.4 yuan.
\newline

Work out the cost of this pot in dollars.
\newline

(b) Sean records the maximum and minimum temperatures, in °C, at the museum.
\newline
Some of the results for one week are shown in the table.
\newline

\begin{table}[h]
\begin{tabular}{l|ccccccc}
Day & Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri & Sat & Sun \\
\hline
Maximum temperature (°C) & 8 & 12 & 15 & 14 & 11 & 7 & 4 \\
Minimum temperature (°C) & -5 & -2 & -4 & -1 & 3 &  &  \\
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

(i) (c) Find the difference between the maximum temperature and the minimum temperature on Wednesday.
\newline

(d) The minimum temperature on Saturday was 2 °C higher than the minimum temperature on Monday.
\newline

Find the minimum temperature on Saturday.
\newline

(e) In this week the range of temperatures was 23 °C.
\newline

Find the minimum temperature on Sunday.
\newline

(f) These are the opening times for the museum.
\newline

Monday to Friday 09 00 to 17 00 \\
Saturday and Sunday 10 00 to 16 00

During opening hours the museum has 4 security guards working.
\newline
Each guard works a maximum of 30 hours each week.
\newline

Work out the smallest number of guards needed each week.
\newline

(g) The entry price to the museum is \$18.
\newline
This price is increased by 28\%.
\newline

Find the increased entry price.
\newline

stem.content:
Sean is the manager of a museum.
\newline

_rawBody:
Sean is the manager of a museum.

\begin{parts}
\item He buys a Chinese pot costing 1200 yuan.
The exchange rate is \$1 = 6.4 yuan.

Work out the cost of this pot in dollars.

\AnswerLine[][\$]
\Marks{1}

\item Sean records the maximum and minimum temperatures, in °C, at the museum.
Some of the results for one week are shown in the table.

\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{l|ccccccc}
Day & Mon & Tue & Wed & Thu & Fri & Sat & Sun \\
\hline
Maximum temperature (°C) & 8 & 12 & 15 & 14 & 11 & 7 & 4 \\
Minimum temperature (°C) & -5 & -2 & -4 & -1 & 3 &  &  \\
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\begin{subparts}
\item Find the difference between the maximum temperature and the minimum temperature on Wednesday.

\AnswerLine[][°C]
\Marks{1}

\item The minimum temperature on Saturday was 2 °C higher than the minimum temperature on Monday.

Find the minimum temperature on Saturday.

\AnswerLine[][°C]
\Marks{1}

\item In this week the range of temperatures was 23 °C.

Find the minimum temperature on Sunday.

\AnswerLine[][°C]
\Marks{1}
\end{subparts}

\item These are the opening times for the museum.

Monday to Friday 09 00 to 17 00 \\
Saturday and Sunday 10 00 to 16 00

During opening hours the museum has 4 security guards working.
Each guard works a maximum of 30 hours each week.

Work out the smallest number of guards needed each week.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{4}

\item The entry price to the museum is \$18.
This price is increased by 28\%.

Find the increased entry price.

\AnswerLine[][\$]
\Marks{2}
\end{parts}

tex:
(a) Jian has a fair spinner in the shape of a regular hexagon.
\newline
The spinner is numbered 2, 2, 3, 4, 4, 5.
\newline

Jian spins the spinner.
\newline

(i) (b) Find the probability that the spinner lands on

an even number,

(c) a number less than 6,

(d) the number 1.
\newline

(e) Mei has two fair square spinners, A and B.
\newline
Spinner A is numbered 1, 2, 2, 4 and spinner B is numbered 3, 3, 4, 5.
\newline

She spins both spinners and adds the two numbers.
\newline

(i) (f) Complete the table to show all the possible outcomes.
\newline

\begin{table}[h]
\begin{tabular}{c|ccccc}
B & 3 & 3 & 4 & 5 \\
\hline
A &  &  &  &  \\
1 & 4 & 4 &  &  \\
2 & 5 & 5 & 6 & 7 \\
2 & 5 & 5 & 6 & 7 \\
4 & 7 & 7 &  &  \\
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

(g) Use the table to write down the probability that the total is

(a) 5,

(b) more than 5.
\newline

(h) Ning has a spinner numbered 1 to 6.
\newline
She spins it 50 times and her results are shown in the table.
\newline

\begin{table}[h]
\begin{tabular}{c|c}
Number on spinner & Frequency \\
\hline
1 & 15 \\
2 & 12 \\
3 & 9 \\
4 & 5 \\
5 & 2 \\
6 & 7 \\
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

(i) (i) Write down the mode.
\newline

(j) Find the median.
\newline

(k) Work out the mean.
\newline

qtype: number properties, reciprocals, standard form, sets
tex:
(a) From this list: 8, 15, 18, 33, 39, 41, 51, 57, 60, 81

(i) (b) write down a factor of 54,

(c) a multiple of 19,

(d) a prime number.
\newline

(e) Write down the reciprocal of 64.
\newline

(f) (i) (g) Write $4.81 \times 10^{-3}$ as an ordinary number.
\newline

(h) Write 75 000 in standard form.
\newline

(i) Calculate $\dfrac{6.3 \times 10^{2}}{7 \times 10^{-3}}$.
\newline
Write your answer in standard form.
\newline

(j) (i) (k) $\xi = \{2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64\}$
$A = \{square\ numbers\}$
$B = \{cube\ numbers\}$

Use this information to complete the Venn diagram.
\newline

(l) On this Venn diagram, shade the region $P \cap Q$.
\newline

qtype: number writing, powers, fraction/percentage equivalence, ordering FDP
tex:
(a) Write one hundred and twenty thousand and twenty in figures.
\newline

(b) Find the value of $3^{4} \times 8^{1}$.
\newline

(c) (i) (d) Write down the fraction of the rectangle that is shaded.
\newline

(e) Find the percentage of the rectangle that is not shaded.
\newline

(f) Write these numbers in order, starting with the smallest.
\newline
$\dfrac{5}{17}$, 27\%, 0.268, $\dfrac{7}{29}$

 ,  ,  , 

(g) Write 0.3728 correct to 1 decimal place.
\newline

(h) Write down the value of $19^{0}$.
\newline

(i) The height, $h$ metres, of a tower is 128 m, correct to the nearest metre.
\newline

Complete the statement about the value of $h$.
\newline

$127.5 \leq h < 128.5$

(j) Find the highest common factor (HCF) of 126 and 180.
\newline

(k) Write down an irrational number with a value between 6 and 7.
\newline

(g) 18, 20, 24, 32, 48, \dots
